assumptions: The declared intragroup dividend has already created a recognised intragroup monetary receivable/payable.
The item is denominated in a currency that creates foreign exchange gains or losses not fully eliminated on consolidation because the group entities involved have different functional currencies.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 allows a recognised asset or liability to be designated in a fair value hedge, and specifically permits foreign currency risk on an intragroup monetary item in consolidation when the FX effects are not fully eliminated.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 includes cash flow hedge accounting for a recognised asset or liability when variability attributable to the hedged risk could affect profit or loss, which makes it a relevant hedge model to consider for the foreign currency exposure.
